findings.note: When the tokenizer returns None (e.g. backslash escapes), the cwd-aware pass returns False and defers to the substring analysis, which is only fail-closed when the full protected path appears literally. A backslash-obfuscated basename after cd still evades denial. This falls under the documented best-effort/non-complete-interpreter non-goal and is not a regression, but the code comment claiming the fallback 'retains its fail-closed behavior' overstates the coverage. Recommend follow-up hardening (SEC-15-class).

mv treats only the final argument as written, so cd <protected-dir> && mv <protected-basename> /tmp/x is allowed even though mv destroys the protected source. This mirrors the pre-existing issue-#62 semantics that T-002 was required to preserve on the shared corpus, so it is out of scope here, but it is a real enforcement gap worth a tracked follow-up.
